What we need even less is a True Lung past his prime... and this power accessible only to the True Lung for the past thousand years...
If we talk of Great Yan's future, then I must say, my outlook is indeed brighter than yours, Grand Commandant...
Has Great Yan these past forty years... lacked for loyal and righteous men?
The Grand Preceptor, Yu Cheng, Gu Quan... the Grand Tutor, and yourself...
My officials are loyal, my soldiers are fearless, my people are principled... Where is this 'weakness hiding under peace and prosperity' you speak of?
As for the future you mentioned, you can take a look in my stead...
Your Majesty... you still insist...
That Hunt of my ancestors' days; the remnants of the bestial Sui plaguing Yan for a thousand years... should end with me.
If we must inevitably spill some blood...
Then the True Lung should be the first one to spill it.
After a short silence, the True Lung walks to the doors. It has been a long time since he has stepped out of the place.
Only an old man remains in the vast palace.
If I cannot dissuade Your Majesty, then I can only strive to serve my duty.
Send word to Yumen, it's time to make a move.
After the brief order, he disconnects the terminal and slowly walks forward.
He passes through the curtain, finally stopping before the throne, closing his eyes and staying in his spot, as if waiting for something...
Until a cough he can no longer suppress bursts from his throat.
The cough is so violent he bends over, and has to grab onto the throne for support.

lines.loc: One day before the full moon, Hour of Hai

lines.x: You came, in the end.
So you were here.
You don't seem surprised at all to see me, though.
Ch'en Hui-chieh is somewhat caught off-guard by her target simply showing up before her.
Still the overbearing posture, still the unfathomable look, as if they are not standing in Baizao's Forbidden City, but in the backyard of his official residence in Lungmen.
The buildings behind him seamlessly blend into his dark coat, as if those palaces are merely an excessively ornate cloak over his shoulders.
So, at least you weren't kidnapped.
I still don't know just who on this land could manage that.
Everything I've done has been of my own volition.
I'll never stop hating that attitude of yours...
Why are you here? Is this that backup plan the Grand Tutor kept telling you to make?
His words should not be followed entirely... the situation should never have developed as he suggested, in the first place.
I have another plan in mind.
Either way, you're safe. So what comes next won't be much of a problem.
And what are you preparing to do?
Ensure you're safe, get you out of Baizao, then...
Ch'en casually waves the writing knife in her right hand, its simple form leaving a light yellow scroll-like afterimage in the air.
...Correct some mistakes made by you and your brother.
I see... so the artifact is with you.
I'm not too surprised.
Hah. And who were you supposing would deliver this writing knife to you?
That's no longer important...
Since you have it, then you ought to know how to use it... and everything else about it.
I was pretty surprised. Every secret I've learned about you since coming to the city has... opened my eyes.
You know, I almost admire your ability to hide so many secrets.
If you truly wish to rectify the mistake I made then, you should follow me.
Follow you...
Where?
Beneath the Forbidden City, to the first Originium.
The writing knife you hold will be of use, and will solve Great Yan's crisis. All will be made right.
Of course, I prefer you hand the knife to me instead. It'll be quicker, and you'll have less trouble to contend with.
But you won't trust me. I'm sure you want to see it all yourself before you'll give up. I don't really mind, though.
Haven't had enough of playing the tragic hero? You're just conveniently skipping over the part where you'd actually lose your life.
We stand before a great crisis... and this is a small cost Yan can afford.
There are no victims in this case. Your work ends here, Superintendent Ch'en.
Don't tell me you forgot. I resigned right in front of you, five years ago.
Though the man standing before me now isn't even fit to be called 'Chief Wei'.
You're telling me to watch you throw your life away, and that's not even the part of your words that's pissing me off the most.
Your sacrifice isn't going to move anyone's heart but your own.
And right now, you don't even have the right to end your life just like that.
Hui-chieh, this is no petty squabble. This matter concerns the fate of Yan and its people!
Of course I know that...
But get over your own ego. The country's fate doesn't fall on your shoulders alone. There are definitely other things you should be doing, besides glorifying your own suicide.
Very well.
You don't have to come with me.
Give me the writing knife and go back to Lungmen.
It's too bad I got two blades here, but I can't lend you either of them.
We don't have any more time to lose, and you still want it your way, Superintendent Ch'en?
I'm not the one insisting on having it my way.
Must you force my hand...?
Fine, show me. Let me see how much your swordplay has grown in the last five years!
That's more like it.
Ch'en Hui-chieh grips the hilt of her sword.
Since she first took up the Chi Xiao, she knew this moment would one day come.
She unsheathes her blade, pointing it at the Baizao autumn breeze blowing between her and Wei Yenwu.
As sword ere shadow's trace...
...Cast off as cast.
Hah, hah...
Do you... intend to use Heaven's Eye.
...Scared?
Skillful, but not yet the peak of perfection.' The Grandmaster once said of your swordplay.
Your skill has improved much in five years, and you've surpassed my expectations in comprehending Chi Xiao's techniques.
But you are still one step away from Heaven's Eye.
The eye of Heaven sees all and nothing.
Only when the clouds are parted is Heaven's wrathful eye revealed.
Let your heart guide the sword, never looking back. Turn around, and the sword loses its sharpness, harming the user.
I was only reminding you of its form... but I see you remembered it all.
But you should be well aware a fog remains in your heart, so the technique is beyond your ability.
Drop your sword. I especially don't want to go that far with you.
Will you use it though, if I don't give you the writing knife?
...Just like you did thirty five years ago?
Don't force my hand, Hui-chieh.
When you walked out of the Forbidden City forty years ago, when you decided whether Tal and her father would live or die, did you do it with the technique's ideals... 'never looking back'?
Hui-chieh!
If that's what Chi Xiao's final technique demands, then I shall never be able to unleash it.
Trial. Justice.
Good and evil, right and wrong—they aren't weights you can easily tell which is better when placed on two sides of a scale... 'Don't judge a life until its end' was never a positive term to use.
The same goes for people, and even more so. They shouldn't be used and then unceremoniously abandoned.
Workers in the Victorian siege, warriors of the Ursus tundra, and even the casino bosses in Dossoles. All of them understand that.
So you are right. I won't use Heaven's Eye. I can't learn it. It's not my bladework.
What are you getting at?
More than keeping a resolute will to abandon everything, I'd rather pick the path of knowing what I find impossible.
Hui-chieh... you still don't understand!
You're right. I don't.
I don't actually understand what happened that night, whether your father actually died by Chi Xiao or something more sinister.
I don't understand what you were thinking. I do know Edward and Tal both bore the curse of their bloodline, while I could only watch from the sidelines.
I don't understand why Mother resented you, Father, and even me on her deathbed.
I'm only clear on one thing. And Tal knows it too.
Apologies from the dead are meaningless. If there are people you want to apologize to, then tell them you're sorry while you're still alive.
Otherwise, you're just a coward consumed by guilt to the brim, putting on a heartless and stoic act for a ridiculous facade.
I won't let you keep making the same mistakes.
There is no real merit in winning a verbal fight.
If you truly have a strike that surpasses Heaven's Eye, you shall indeed become a legendary Yanese swordswoman.
Show me. Prove with my blood that you are right.
I'll show you...
Ch'en silently switches Chi Xiao to her left hand, and wields the writing knife with her right.
I'll prove that if the dilemma itself is fundamentally wrong, you'll never get the right answer, no matter which side you take.
I'll prove that before we reach an inevitable dead end, we can take a step back and consider other options.
A choice that requires no sacrifice or abandonment, but allows all things to thrive.
You plan to use Chi Xiao's swordplay with the writing knife?
You said the techniques of Chi Xiao were all about one's state of mind, so it doesn't really matter which sword you use, right?
Uncle...
I still remember your last words to me when I left Lungmen.
Wei Yenwu's fingers stretch and bend before finally settling into a sharp form.
A gale swirls around him, blowing at his simple robes.
Hui-chieh, I'll respect your determination.
I won't hold back.
Heaven's Eye. Wrathful eye revealed, never looking back.
Even without a physical blade, his fierce form remains like merciless sunlight, never to return once it has shone.
Remorse does not linger on his fingers nor in his sword's aura. Yet it pervades the air, like mountains of withered leaves in late autumn.
Hesitation, regret, self-blame.
She knows why the old man before her wields his blade. His pent-up emotions do not have to be expressed in speech for her to empathize with.
Then, like a sigh, she thrusts the writing knife out.
After the truth comes to light, a judge's long exclamation often feels heavier than the verdict itself.
After the sigh, the sharp wind rolling around him dissipates like a fading dream, leaving nothing but a solitary palace, a desolate square, and the two standing in silence before it.
So this is... the swordplay you meant.
After I came to Baizao, I heard some stories about my mother.
When you left the city, she followed you to Lungmen. I never understood, but now, what other reason was there...?
She wanted to convince her brothers, her own two siblings, to make up.
And now, I think she was probably even more naive than I am.
You met someone from the House of Ch'en...
...I owe your parents and them an apology.
There are some things that should be resolved with blood, but this matter—this family matter that has entangled your entire life and my decades doesn't have to be one of them.
You felt regret in the end.
You dared only hold the form of Heaven's Eye, but not its spirit. You know what comes after remorse.
The Heaven's Eye you wielded decades ago was before regret reached your heart. You saw all and nothing, never looking back.
And when you finally felt it, it gnawed at your heart nonstop.
You win... Of course, you're right.
But this way you stake everything on a war that cannot be controlled. Now countless Yanese soldiers and civilians have their lives on the line, not just you and I.
Those with power and privilege love hiding their selfishness with big numbers and scary consequences. But obviously, there are plenty of other choices besides your death.
There are many who stand with us, proxies who have joined forces with Yan, your own brother and even, if there really is no other choice to take up the responsibility, there's me.
Let's finish the conversation from earlier.
...'The path before you is long and hard. But I know you will master it.'
I thought for a long time about what you meant by that 'will'. When I found the answer, I was loath to believe the conclusion it led to.
But now, seeing you in such a pathetic state, I can only believe it...
From that moment on, when you placed your unfulfilled hopes on my shoulders, you were already old.
You're old. You can't even harden your heart into stone as you did in your prime. You want to make amends, pay your debts. So you choose... to die.
Death won't let you make up for all you've done, death won't preserve your last shred of dignity. Death will only leave a final knot in your relationship that can never be untangled.
So don't do it... don't do what Tal did.
Is she...?!
If you want to ask me 'Is she doing well?', then I'll tell you. No. Not at all.
But you should rejoice in the fact I'm willing to talk about her with anybody... with you.
What's that?
...The horns signaling the attack. It's not from Baizao, but outside... from Yumen.
Yumen's about to attack the Tomb of Sui?
The Grand Commandant has issued the command, but whether or not the troops move is up to Zuo Xuanliao.
He cannot hold on forever, though. Yumen's troops are not his personal army.
...I should go.
To do what?
Our family matters have to be resolved, but those incarnations of the Feranmut, the same goes for them too. They'll try their best, and I'm willing to trust them.
And that Grand Commandant...
I can kind of guess what's on his mind. He's not selfish, but he thinks his judgements are logical and realistic.
Disasters caused by people like him are usually the worst ones.
Be it for Lungmen or what's left of the House of Ch'en, I should try and win whatever peace is possible for Yan.
I know you will.
Right.
Before I go, and if you want, help me think up a name for the move I pulled off. A name like Heaven's Eye that would get a young me all excited.
...Heaven's Plaint.
Heaven's Plaint. Doesn't that sound a little old-timey?
Surely you don't have to remind me of my age every five minutes.
Even so, there are some things that I must do myself.
Such as?
This time, I will do something that won't leave me with regrets.
((dummy text))
The mastermind who turned Yumen upside down, and an actual Feranmut.
Zuo Le grips his blade and swallows hard.
But the other party does not seem to pay him any mind, instead looking towards Mo Yi.
And where is the thing you promised me?
I... didn't find it...
Then there is no meaning in leaving you alive—
With no hesitation, a long, narrow blade manifests in Ya's hand, dazzling the other two with its silver glint.
W-Wait!
Kill me, and you'll never know where Bu Fan is!
What?!
Mo Yi, what exactly—
Faced with Zuo Le's angry stare, Mo Yi can only turn her head away.
Let me ask you first... what are you planning to do with Bu Fan once you have it?
Just kill Sui, or do you want the whole city, or even all Yan to also die alongside your grudge?
That is of no concern to you.
I only promised to help you exact revenge against the bestial Sui... but before I give it to you, I need you to promise me something.
Your rage can only be directed at Sui... and once you're done with your vengeance, you'll leave Yan.
Absurd.
Why should I promise you anything?
No particular reason... but I'm not giving it to you unless you agree.
You've exhausted my patience with your life.
Zuo Le steps in almost instinctively, and takes the blow. He realizes once more just how much power a Feranmut holds.
All he feels is his insides churning as blood spurts from his mouth.
Zuo Le—
The Sui Regulator will not allow you to harm anyone, even if she is a Shanhaizhong—
Ridiculous.
It is this very human game that I hate—
A light flick of Her longsword, and the Candleholder's own blade flies from his hand, as his throat meets the edge of Her blade.
The whereabouts of Bu Fan, or his life.
No way... You can't...
...Don't hurt him! I'll tell you!
The Shanhaizhong takes out an elaborate box from her pocket, and opens it to reveal an ancient map within.
That's from the Sky Mirror Pavilion, when did you—
...Sorry...
Lord Zuo... I admit to the crime of deceiving you over and over again...
Dare I ask you to trust me... and if a chance comes, to let me be the good one and make amends?
You mustn't——!
Ignoring the Candleholder's futile attempt, Mo Yi hands the map over to Ya, the latter merely sweeping a glance at it before the parchment turns into ashes.
Enough. I did what you wanted... you should leave.
You tricked me once, and still hope for me to trust you?
You shall come with me, until I find Bu Fan.
Ya raises Her longsword, but does not point it at the two, instead slicing a rift into the air...
Don't just stand there, run—
Mo Yi is swallowed by the rift, leaving only withered autumn leaves all around Zuo Le.
What... did you do...?
...Stop.
Where are you going...?
I think it beneath me to make another move on someone I have no business with.
If you cherish your life, then leave.
Zuo Le silently picks up his blade from the floor and steps forward into a fighting stance.
Countless times he has done this, against formidable foes he thought he could never defeat.
So you have a death wish.
As if merely following the proxy's murderous intent, a chilling autumnal breeze blows by, and sharp withered leaves cut tiny wounds on Zuo Le.
But right after, he sees something he can hardly believe—the dead leaves turn into flowers and green, and the piercing autumn wind is gradually replaced by a gentle spring breeze.
Who dares?!
Why do we always run into each other when a fight's about to start?
This encounter is no different from the one a thousand years ago.
Our former foes are gone, as are our dear friends...
To draw your blade for vengeance now—how is it not a futile grasp at the past?
You two...?!
The proxy is loath to address Her kin by the names humans gave Them, yet She realizes—how else are They to call one another?
Ya detests these names.

lines.st: Hou.
Ju.
So you were both alive all along?!

lines.x: As you can see, we are no illusions or walking corpses, so yes.
But this is no place for chit-chat... Can your hidden realm welcome us as guests?
I thought It and I were the last of our kind on this land.
How have you survived these last thousand years... and why have you come here today?
Do you want to stop me as well?!
We don't intend to make things difficult for you... but are you aware just how much trouble you've added to our plates with your awakening and return to Yan?
Since that day, I've been followed by hundreds of eyes, even when I'm just casually angling for some silverfin on the Dusk River.
Staring at your decrepit shell. Hah, those ants sure are experts at wasting time.
Ruined mounts and streams, bodies weak and sick. That's all we are now, and you're no exception.
Weak and sick...
Yet enough strength to leave another wound on It that will never heal!
...Is that the only thing you've gained from your long journey?
After this millennium-long journey, all that remains in your head is wanting to settle the score between you two?
Seriously, your smarts are not up to par...
Anger, humiliation, discontent, confusion. You came back on account of these, and you use them as excuses to level your blade against your own kind.
Humanity gave a name to this obsession born of anger—they call it 'vengeance'.
You proudly claim to be a Feranmut and look upon 'human emotions' with contempt...
Yet you've changed. When did you become so obsessed with the boards and games humans play?
And when did you primordial beings pick up the rhetoric used by those crass pests?
Fooling humans, hunting our own kind... when have we ever suffered such humiliation?
When have we suffered... such a betrayal?
Vengeance? Boards? Games?
This is a war between It and me that has yet to end!
Lands shorn of life, mountains split asunder, rivers choked with crimson... The war at the dawn of time is not worth my remembrance.
Eons ago before you granted these insects your cunning, they had long embarked on the wrong path.
Now look at you, taking on a form similar to theirs—comical, ugly, broken, unbearable... you make me laugh!
And you, Hou. The ancient bestial Hou, the 'nature' of antiquity—you who once floated between heaven and earth have too learned how to live like an ant.
One can live as they wish in this realm. Rafting, swimming, fowl-watching, fincatching... You're not me, so how would you understand the joy I derive from this land?
You find joy in dragging your tail through the mud?!
Seasons change, life and death come and go, joy is but a speck, and sorrow the same... to me, these joys and sorrows are but floating weeds on the Dusk River. Where the wind blows, I go.
If one day a finbeast devours me whole, I shall drift freely for ten thousand miles with the current; if a fowlbeast snatches me, I shall be lifted up into the highest heavens.
Will you raise your weapon against the wind, a fin, a fowlbeast?
To be used or cast aside is of the times; to act or to hide is of my own will.
That is what Ju and I have come here to tell you.
Continue your reckless act, and you shall only meet your end—A Feranmut's end, with your shell no more, as only ash and dust.
Simply put, we pity you, our kin.

lines.st: Pity?! For your kin?!

lines.x: The two of you only leave me with shame.
Kin, you say?
The two of you, as primordial manifestations of 'nature' and 'rules', are groveling at my feet to surrender the ages?!
Puny, weak, filthy, low... What grants you two rotting corpses the right to call yourselves my kin?
I'll lose my body, and end as only ash and dust?
Heh, the destruction in your eyes is to me simply a return.

lines.st: A return.
To the sleepless chaos, the indistinguishable dawn.

lines.x: So what if I am reduced to nothing? So what if I am consigned to eternal nothingness?

lines.st: This realm is but a coffin with no borders in sight. A thousand years I have waited, and the only thing missing is a dark red pattern—
Soaked, painted, lined, and carved...
A breathtaking pattern made from Its flesh and cries.

lines.x: You take this realm as a coffin... Is it then not a waste, preserving this vast realm when only death awaits you?
I dwell within a leaf, but see an entire realm within... All things arise from the mind, and it should stay free and unfettered.
*sigh*...Is it this realm that confines you?
The land trembles from its master's rage. The chill of the cold, jade mountains grows even sharper from Her raging fury.
But the old man merely taps his long staff on the water's surface, and a plum blossom blooms on the river that has never melted.
Hah... so you wish to face me in my own realm?
Arrow's nocked.
Yes, use your arrow, your staff, your pest-like forms to smash all that you see as coffins!
Then come with me in your sly disciple's stead, and take a good look for yourselves...
Witness how this thousand-year old feud shall finally end.
Ya's figure vanishes into thin air, leaving only Ju and Hou on the frozen river.
I see stopping the war is no longer possible.
Are we really going to destroy this realm She has preserved for so many years?

Pity for your kin' really struck a nerve with Ya, but isn't that why we've been trying to make Her see the error of Her ways? How can we drive the knife even deeper when She's already wallowing in self-abandonment?
So from now on, must we only watch from the sidelines as Wang's plot unfolds?
We guided that lass once. We saved that Candleholder, and even stood in for that precious disciple of yours. We've done our part for Wang.
He probably knows we can't stop Ya, which is why he even let us involve ourselves in this troublesome affair in the first place.
He really is like It. Only, he has a bit more...
What's done is done. Let's just rest on this river and partake in the scenery from a millennium ago.
Time's running out for It as well.
Aiyo...
Wait, I'm back?
And you're alive too! Where's Ya?
I saw an old man and a... person covered with machinery.
They stopped Ya, then they all vanished.
Someone covered in metal? Don't tell me—
Damn it, I was so close! Why am I the only one who hasn't seen Him!
Forget it. Since He's already here, I'm sure I'll get another chance to meet Him when this is all done with.
Thanks... for saving me earlier.
Saving you?
Haha... Without my convincing performance, she would've thought my actual intel was fake, right? Wouldn't that be a real shame?
...What?
What were you planning, divulging the location of Bu Fan to that proxy?
You keep asking me what mission the Old Tianshi gave me, right?
Man... you wouldn't believe me anyway, maybe when you become that Sui Regulator Supervisor whatchamacallit...
...What was that?
From far away, a bugle horn sounds, sending Mo Yi in a frantic search for its source, but to Zuo Le, it could not be more familiar.
That's Yumen's call to march.
Yumen... is preparing for an assault on the Tomb of Sui.
So soon?! The bestial Sui hasn't woken up, right—
The bestial Sui has yet to wake, the city is in chaos, and the Sui Regulator still hasn't fulfilled its duties... how could they possibly issue the order to attack the tomb right now?
Something must have happened inside Yumen... I should—
Well then, get going!
But we have to take that token to the Tomb of Sui!
After a moment of silence, Zuo Le speaks with hesitation.
Can I... trust you...?
Just shut up... and leave it to me.
...Since you saved my life earlier.
...Stay safe.

lines.loc: The Day of the Full Moon

lines.x: Baizao outskirts, near the Tomb of Sui.
An old man stands alone amidst dry grass and withered poplars, silently staring at where the barrens end, in the direction of Baizao's urban core.
Gah... it's been so cold this autumn.
What are you doing out here all alone, on a day this cold?
Waiting for someone.
Oh... have you eaten?
Here, I have a bag of buns with me. Fill your belly a little?
We can eat once everyone's here.
If we're gonna wait for more, then I better prepare a proper meal, buns won't do...
...Do you think they'll come back?
What do you think?
Of course I think they will!
I know we rarely come together, we always argue whenever we do meet; we don't share the same hobbies and have conflicting personalities...
But it's times like these that I believe our hearts are all as one!
Though, even if everyone comes back, can we even...?
*sigh*...
I'll wait with you then.

lines.loc: The Day of the Full Moon

lines.x: The sand blots out the sun.
Zuo Le crouches within Yumen's shadow, looking out at the Wind Aegis, the sand sluices, the distinct silhouette of the cannons...
The entire city slowly rumbles towards the Tomb of Sui.
Yumen really did receive marching orders.
But... why would His Majesty order an attack on Sui before It has awakened?
After this moment of hesitation, Yumen is almost right on top of the crouching Candleholder.
Soon, there will be no more room for him to move forward. There are only the city walls, jutting up perpendicularly from the ground.
You wish to leave via the sand sluices? Have you perfected your qinggong yet? Not to mention, once you head out...
...Never mind. You will see for yourself.
Do not think that your fall will excuse you from punishment. It would be one thing if you caused this trouble on your own, but you dragged Qingping into it!
General, it was just because Zuo Le—
Do not make excuses for him.
He should have thought of how to come back up before he jumped down! All that bravery, but all he knows is how to fight, fight, fight, and how to make others nearly die of worry!
Back to your place, and once your wounds have healed somewhat, come and receive your punishment.
The city walls are only ten-some meters away from Zuo Le.
And so he takes a great leap forward, the qinggong others praise him so much for is now the only thing he can rely on.
Though the sand sluices have not started up, the Wind Aegis has been fully erected, its smooth walls nearly impossible to find purchase on. A single slip would mean a horrible death.
Halfway up, and there are still battlements he can use to support himself. But to go further would be as strapping thousands of catties to himself.
Faster...
As he passes through the yellow sand, the young man sees the figures of soldiers manning the walls.
Many of them seem to have already forgotten just how long they have stood atop these ramparts.
However, with the sudden blast of a horn, they have now been tasked with spending the rest of their lives on this new hunt.
Faster!
The higher he climbs, the clearer his thinking becomes, the steadier his steps.
Even so, the final stretch of the walls is no stroll down a garden path.
Still fifteen kilometers from the Tomb, can't see anything about Sui's movements from here.
Still no news from the Sui Regulator... Did the higher-ups really give the order to open fire?
—No, I would never! Yes! Understood!
Who—
My apologies.
Don't move.
Help! There's a spy—
This area's long evacuated. Shout all you like.
How would you know? What're you up to?
I am an imperial envoy. I have come to investigate why Yumen is taking action without permission.
An imperial envoy? Didn't they already come by earlier? You think you can pretend to be one of them while sneaking around?
(Already came by earlier?)
Of course I am an envoy of the court. Don't you recognize these clothes?
Beastshit you are! Those are nothing like the clothes they wore! They had proper court mandarin uniforms on!
...The Ministry of War.
How do you know that?
Not long after the Ministry of War's envoys came to Yumen, the order to deploy was passed down, correct?
Has General Zuo made a public appearance since then? Before any great battle, he would always personally beat the watchtower drum to inspire morale.
...Are you really from the court?
Of course I am.
These marching orders did seem really strange, but... if it wasn't General Zuo, then who could have issued them?
...I see.
I've already untied your wrists. Returning to the tower now will most likely raise suspicion, so I suggest you wait here a while. Goodbye.
The soldier rubs his eyes with his numb hands, wondering why the young man, leaping away with his qinggong, seemed somewhat familiar.
...Father.
Zuo Le?! How did you get into the city?!
I climbed up through the sand sluices—No, that's not important right now...
I am just glad you are safe...
Where are the men from the Ministry of War, Father?
You... Why are you trying to find them?
The orders to march upon the Tomb of Sui and fire at it, if they were not your orders, then they must be theirs, correct?
Otherwise, why would you be here alone, like you are under house arrest?
Silence! Do not presume to comment on military affairs—
Father! The Grand Commandant is doing all this because he has decided to overstep His Majesty! He is vainly trying to fire on the Tomb to start a war!
The Grand Commandant has formed a cabal, and will sit and watch as Baizao is overrun by scriptgeists, in collusion with the Supervisor of the Sui Regulator.

Not only that, but he tried to seize the proxy's artifact within the Sky Mirror Pavilion, and also force Chief Executive Wei of Lungmen to attack Sui using some secret technique. None of these plans worked, which is why he commanded Yumen to fire upon the Tomb.
His Majesty never gave such an order! The ones given to Yumen to move and fire are false! Do not be deceived, Father!
He did all these things to wage war upon Sui...?
Surely you believe me, Father.
...Yes. How could I not?
But know this: the Tomb lies in front of Yumen, and Baizao behind it. No matter what orders are given, the city must not fall into disarray!
Those above want to advance, those below want to observe. When these two forces clash, they will cause panic in the city. If Sui truly were to awaken, the soldiers of Yumen will be decimated!
But these soldiers, do they know they fight for the Grand Commandant's false orders, and not the will of Yan?
Before a march, their general usually pounds the drum atop the walls to inspire morale. Have you done so, General Zuo?
Without this encouragement from their commander, the men's spirits have not been lifted. On the contrary, they are confused. Are they truly braving Sui's claws to protect Baizao?
Or are they the inevitable first sacrifices at the altar for the belligerent Yan the Grand Commandant envisions?
Zuo Le—
Father, you are not only General Zuo of Yumen, but also the Lord Exorcist of Yan—
We just received reports of a suspicious individual infiltrating Yumen. It appears he stole into your residence.
What crimes should your son be charged for his trespass into military fortifications, General Zuo?
Just as Zuo Xuanliao is about to open his mouth to reply, Zuo Le steps forward, drawing the sword at his waist halfway.
I assume you heard all I said earlier, sir. Considering you can still say such things after that, you must be in league with the Grand Commandant.
You're talking nonsense, you insolent child!
Are you going to turn a blind eye to your son's disrespect, General Zuo?
Sir, I truly must wonder, why is the Grand Commandant is so eager to wage war against Sui?
Still questioning your orders even now, General?

With Terra in such turmoil, the entirety of the court unanimously passed the Twenty-Eight Policies. But outside those twenty-eight, there were two more hidden policies, the first of which is that the Sui menace must be eliminated!
If we do not eliminate It now, surely you do not mean to wait until our enemies from abroad are at the gates?!
I shall tell you the truth, General. It is not only the Grand Commandant who supports this policy! More than half the court mandarins do as well!
...And what does the Grand Tutor say? What does His Majesty say?
It is not your place to ask!
It is also a question I have been meaning to ask, sir.
Besides the Grand Commandant and the Ministry of War... The mandarins of the Six Ministries and His Majesty, do they know all the facts? Have they agreed to all this?
These are not matters you should concern yourself with, Zuo Xuanliao! You are merely a general!
So they do not know.
Soldiers must heed orders, and Yumen cannot be allowed to fall into chaos. Thus, I am willing to be patient, and watch as you replace me before battle is met...
But if what comes next is an age of strife, would your actions not be drawing Yan into panic, creating new conflict within?!
I may be a military man, but I am also the Lord Exorcist of Yan. Forgive me, but I cannot receive such arbitrary orders!
...You've put your own head on the chopping block. Guards, arrest this man!
It seems you really are all preparing to revolt.
Who goes there?!

I am Taihe, Monitor Censor of the Department of Discipline and Supervision. I happened to be here for rest and recuperation, and I must say, I did not expect to find the Ministry of War acting without authorization, causing unrest within Yumen.
You need not mind me. I will not interfere in your affairs.
But so long as I leave this room alive, do not believe the events that have taken place today can be treated like they never happened.
The Ministry of War's envoy and his men freeze in place.
...Enough.
Here and now, I am still the warden of Yumen. If the Grand Commandant wishes to court-martial me after the matter, then whether I am to be sacked, exiled or otherwise, I will submit.
But as for now, out of my way. I must go see my men.
Zuo Le fully draws his sword, shielding his father as the two of them walk out the room.
Those so-called envoys are now in detention, Father.
Good. Next—
Grave news, General! The city's communications systems... They've been severed!
What?!
It seems those Ministry men picked up where the wind was blowing before they barged into the meeting room, so they performed some sabotage. We're relying on runners right now.
The mobility layer still works fine, but there are artillery emplacements all across Yumen, and the order has already been passed down. The orders the men receive keep changing...
Begin repairs immediately, and do everything you can to stop the cannons from firing.
Yes, sir!
Taihe, relay my orders to the mobility layer.
It shall be done.
Zuo Le, I shall entrust the emplacements within the deepest regions of the city to you.
Yes, sir!
One more thing.
You've done well today.
...Damn these monsters! There's no end to them!
We can't... hold out much longer...
Another twenty-four hours passed, and the fighting continues at the defensive line against the geists in Baizao.
The strange geists are like an endless tide, wave after wave crashing against their defenses. Though they may be cut down, they always reappear, stronger than before.
There is no doubt. The force the defenders face now are the 'god-hunters' of days long past.
Keep your chins up! The battle is not yet over! This is no time to admit defeat!
The sword in her hand is bent, her armor slowly but steadily getting covered in a mass of scars.
His Majesty decrees—

All soldiers, whether Patrol Battalion, Yulin Guard, or otherwise, are to gradually withdraw from Baizao's core plate. Join with the troops stationed outside the city, and pull back defenses to a ten-kilometer perimeter around Baizao.
Pull back our defenses?! That's ridiculous! The Forbidden City is right behind us!
Don't tell me His Majesty is prepared to abandon even the core plate?
I double-checked, those really are His Majesty's orders!
Your Majesty...
Where are you right now?

lines.st: After forty years, he steps into this secret chamber once more.
He still remembers the emotions he felt the first time he set foot in here—
Remorse, confusion, resentment, terror...

Forty years has been long enough to smooth them away,

long enough to grind away at all feeling, aside from the one known as helplessness.
Forty years, passed in a flash.

lines.x: If you are still unwilling to bow your head to Yan, then I alone shall bear your thousand years of rage.
The sin of disloyalty... I shall also stamp out alone.
This millennium of grudges and grievances shall end here. From now on, Yan will begin a new chapter in its history...
Why wait for Heaven to bless the nation? There is only... the triumph of man over nature.
The True Lung looks towards the blazing radiance before him, as if he can see a pair of giant, ancient eyes burning within, burning with a great fury.
All he waits for is the signal that Sui has fully awakened before he martyrs himself.
The heart-pounding, golden light has nearly faded away. All that remains is an afterglow that falls into Wang's palm.
Within that very instant, a flood of jumbled, shattered memories flows past his eyes. With all his might, he reaches in to grab hold of a fragment, a splinter, anything with a trace of her.
For a hundred-and-twenty years, she has never appeared in his dreams.
Finally, finally, he grasps onto something that survived—
A record of a game.
Not some legendary game handed down the ages, but rather, a casual game between brother and sister as they chatted.
He flips the record over, wanting to see what was written in that message she left behind that is missing from his memory...
He finally reads it.

lines.st: You are a pessimistic one, brother, always thinking that the heart of humanity is unknowable, an unending flow of evil. Only through careful calculation and reasoning can it be understood.

I beg to differ. I believe humanity to be fundamentally good, evil deeds merely a covering of the heart. Thus, I believe there is no need to attempt to decipher it. Simply read the history books and learn the ways of the world, and you shall understand through analogy.

You knew I was lazing about, brother, which is why you came to give me a 'bored' written atop your board. Even before you placed your first stone with that blatant scowl on your face, I knew you had come to poke fun at me.

Both interpretation and analogy are fine. These split paths shall meet again, at a summary of the human heart. Of our family, only you and I walk this self-defeating road, always wanting to know the hearts of men.
Perhaps, when we both obtain the same answer...

lines.x: The letter stops here.
The weiqi player stares at the exquisite handwriting for a long, long time, seemingly forgetting the life-and-death struggle he is currently locked in.
Split paths shall meet again.
Split paths. She is long gone.
But to meet again...?
The game nears its end.

lines.st: How amusing. She was the first of you to raise the flag of rebellion against me.
I must admit, over these past thousand years, I truly did fear her growing power, even if only for a moment.
But her plan was defeated by her lack of cunning, defeated by the 'unknowable heart of humanity'.
What is even more preposterous is that she chose to give you a chance to live...

lines.x: ...She has never once been defeated.
Through her own force of will, she bought the rest of us the chance to live.
Because she believed that after her death... we would be able to surpass you...

lines.st: Haha... Hahahaha...!
How pitiful!
Are you actually feeling... regret?
You came to do battle with me, so determined to die, but you could not even triumph over your own weakness.
Do you care for her?

lines.x: Yes. I care for her.
I once thought I did not... I thought the outcome of our battle should be the only thing I cared for.
Until I learned something later on.
The hearts of men. You, a wild beast... will never understand.
And so... You are doomed to pay the price.

lines.st: Enough!
So this is to be your final struggle... This is the result of all your careful planning and preparation for a hundred years, this amusement that you now present to me?
Things will not end any different from last time.
The only difference is that now, you are even weaker than before!
Or do you mean to say that, with her choice, killing me will be akin to killing her, which is why you have not the will to make even a single move?
Pitiful! Pathetic! Laughable!
But no matter... None of it matters.
We can wait here. There is time to wait.
Let us see which of your so-called siblings shall come charging in to die in your stead this time.

lines.x: The haggard weiqi player slowly rises from in front of the board, only a few black stones left within his bowl.
His movements are slow and stiff, so stiff as to seem like he had been sat there, withering away for the last hundred-and-twenty years.
As you lay there, half-asleep, half-awake, you turned the void within your body into a puppet, causing all sorts of trouble these past few centuries.

You decided that puppet should walk in the dark, while we walked in the light. And so at first, he tottered as he learned to walk, then he secluded himself to gather his strength, and finally, he ran amok.
But you forgot...
When all is said and done, there is only one of him.
If I had stormed in alone to that game a hundred-and-twenty years ago, having forgotten how wide the board is, and how suffused it was with your might...
Then this time, it is his turn to have too much to do. He must cover all of Yan... and even lands beyond, dealing with black stone after black stone.
This time... Will you still be able to see through my stratagem?

lines.st: Hah...
You tore yourself into a hundred-and-eighty-one pieces, nearly driving yourself to madness, and you believed this would be enough to fool me?
What of it?

lines.x: 'What of it'?
A gravely-wounded Feranmut, forced to sleep and recuperate, having to play a game with humanity while Its thoughts are trapped in unending nightmare...
Such depletion will cut away at the root of your life.
All your bluff and bluster cannot hide it from me. You are growing weak.

lines.st: And what of it?!
All you have done is walk the same road the first to leave me walked, doing all you can to delay my awakening.
But do you truly believe you are awake?
By killing me in every memory, every fantasy, will you be able to throw off the shackles of dream?

lines.x: How could the clash between he and It be limited to these confrontations?
Countless possibilities, countless imaginings, countless conclusions.

Even more things that have transcended cause and effect gently fall through the Tomb of Sui like snow. The proxy looks through each and every snowflake, seeing a game that leads to his death in all of them.
Enough.

lines.st: Finally...
You finally wish to put down that ridiculous game and bare steel against me?
Haha, hahahaha.... Yes, good!
...This shall be my last bit of sport before I awake.

lines.x: ...Finally.
The one who chose White. My reflection in the mirror. Its nightmare.
How difficult it is to meet with you.
Urkh—
Pathetic... Just as I expected. The exact same ending, once again.
You were not bad entertainment, by my standards.
But let us end it here... I must also thank you for this grand... gift you have given me.
With your strength, I shall be able to fully awaken, and devour the power of you twelve whole.
The strength you have gathered over the past thousand years shall be my nutrients, and I shall become strong, far, far stronger than I was a thousand years ago.
I shall become a true god! I shall rule over this land, and lead all those weak insects in breaking through the curtain separating us from the stars, stepping over the ultimate darkness!
With your combined strength... I shall see and obtain that future.
And you, as a part of me, may use my eyes to see it.
Foolish... dreamer...
...Why resist? You will be like that consciousness you call a 'younger sister', returning to where you should be, returning to the primal chaos you came from.
The dream you twelve have been stubbornly chasing after shall be realized within that chaos.
No need for hellish preparations. No need for wasteful schemes. No need for fragile freedom.
You will be me. You will be us.
A great, illogical terror intrudes upon the proxy's mind. His heartbeat quickens, as cold sweat drips down his brow. His body dearly wishes to leap away, but he cannot even swivel his eyeballs.
A blessing.
Split consciousnesses returning to the whole would be the cruelest of blessings.
Abandon your desire for hardship, abandon your worthless obsessions, abandon those duties you bear, abandon those clumsy emotions you learned from wretched humanity.
Abandon that which consumes you. Embrace that which nourishes you.
Suffering must be eliminated. You shall become a keepsake within my great consciousness, a futile... yet respectable memento of the past.
Suffering... eliminated?
Pain seeps down from his skin into his marrow. Terror spreads across his body from his backbone. What the proxy can see slowly begins to blur.
Close your eyes.

lines.st: Yes, close them. You do not need your own, for my eyes are your eyes.
No need to hesitate... Have you not thought this from the very beginning?
The mortal realm does not seem as interesting as your siblings said it was—
...Hmph. Why is this rebellious heart of yours so hard to tame?
No matter. Simply yet another tangled consciousness to slowly digest.
From this thousand-year-long dream...
...I shall finally awaken.
No...

lines.x: It thought It had crushed that shard of consciousness...
But It does not sense Its scattered strength from a hundred-and-twenty years ago flow back into Its body.

lines.st: What does this mean...
Why... am I still in the dream?!

lines.x: The poet lifts the wine cup in her hand, but not to offer it to the heavens, but rather, to pour it upon the ground.
The wine falls, splashing across the yellow soil, becoming a drifting cloud of dust.
Near a winter and a summer since we parted in Shangshu.
Ever since, I had no time to dream of you, for in the lands of man are many things worth dreaming of more.
As for you...
...Hah.
Using dream, using imagination, using things yet to pass to prevail? Verily, you and he are evenly matched, on par with one another...
But I seem to see, amidst your bout, many of my tales emplaced?
...Who could see a gulper of cups, become now a sorrowful drunk?
The poems of others are fine, but at such a juncture, their styles shall not do.
I shall help you change a line. How about...
After rain's end, petals fall beyond count... In wine's embrace, thy freedom is found'?
What say you, sister dearest?
The words of others are fine indeed, and its flow is pleasing, but when you are the one chanting it, it flows far too free, as if you are uninvolved.
Haha, you, me, Jie, Dusk, consensus has always been out of our reach.
Then why not have it be your turn to sing a song, for the me that we share? How about it?
No.
Still unwilling to break your vow?
Jie still has not returned.
However, I shall play for you.
The bloody battlefield is flooded by a peaceful stream. The ripples upon the water quiver, becoming a pair of giant eyes.
Even from within the water, those eyes stare at everything around it. Fierce, pained, near-crazed eyes.
Yet even so, the musician lightly plucks her strings.
Ah, this tune.
It seems that, rather than being unfond of wine, dear Jun, you are simply...
In the middle of the river, the poet toasts alongside the tune, her cheeks flushed red.
She does not chant to match the tune, but with a bright, clear voice. Yet even so, it is in perfect harmony with the tranquil music.
The stars in the sky spin along to the poet and the musician, blending together into a pool of sweet water. From that water appear wines, some bitter, some fragrant.
Even those great eyes begin to show signs of intoxication.
I invite you to share one last cup of wine...
At some point, the tones that started off high and steep gradually deepened, going from near-inaudible to an inescapable bass, lingering within the water.

As for the simple sounds of the instrument, no longer are they quiet and mellow. It is as if only a tenth of the strings were silk, the rest three parts longing, three parts grief, and three parts determination.
But in contrast, the poet's recitation grows lighter, higher, as if a whisper, as if a consolation, as if a torrent of rain. Flicking it away will only serve to wet your clothes.
One low, one high. One disciplined, one unrestrained.
The woman's voice merges with the voice of the instrument, weaving a dream fit to entomb a Feranmut.

lines.st: I invite you to share one last cup of wine...
...For beyond the western pass there are no more of mine...

lines.x: You... You...!
You dare... You dare use such tricks against me?!
The furious Feranmut lifts Its head, attempting to discern the source of the sound, but to no avail.
Its giant, bloodshot eyes first flick towards the proxy, still shackled to the ground. After a moment of quiet, It finally realizes that this dream will not be so easy to break free from.
It does not care if this is the last of the power of the proxy that is here, or interference from proxies far away.
Those great eyes staring at the outside world watch the dream that Ling and Jun weave...
As they watch back.
Within that confrontation that never happened, Its actions, Its moves, Its weak points, even Its psyche. They have seen it all. He has seen it all.

lines.st: Such base deception, such pointless struggles!
Before I have my vengeance on Yan, I should kill you all first, one by one!

lines.x: ...You will not have the chance.
From now on, there is only my... our vengeance against you.
Absurd... Ridiculous!
Your so-called 'sisters' may have deceived me with dreams, and cracked open your shackles, but do you not feel how weak your body is, unfettered though it may be?
This time, I shall crush you, and your miserable schemes!
In that instant of fear and anger, Its enormous body, as great as a mountain, shifts a fraction of an inch.
The entire room begins to shatter into rubble. The great structure that suppressed Sui gradually crumbles, and collapses...
Save me... Save me!
They're back! Those monsters from a hundred years ago are back!
Don't panic, don't panic... Form an orderly line, and walk cautiously...
Why aren't you listening to me?! Oh, what to do, what to do...
Mr. Pu!
Why are there still so many townsfolk who have not evacuated yet?
There are still many geists here—
Stay your hand, Tianshi!
They are not geists born from the turmoil in the Tomb, but rather, the original 'townsfolk'...
They are... the students of the School?
They are my students, yes. I cannot bear to see them lose their lives here, so I beg you two, lend me your aid...
...The Old Tianshi is still guarding the mountain pass. I'm afraid I cannot protect them all on my own.
I shall do my best to hold back the rebellious geists. Yun Qingping, help Mr. Pu in guiding everyone away.
Surely you don't mean to do it alone, Deputy Minister Lin—
It's long past the Garden's closing hours. Why are there still so many tourists here?
Mr. Yi?
You still remember me? And here I was, worried you would forget me, what with you running circles about my garden.
No need for further chatter. You are all lovers of things, which makes us kindred spirits.
You two help this elderly gentleman see everyone out. I can deal with things here myself.
I know you proxies have extraordinary abilities, Mr. Yi, but these are extraordinary times.
I ask that you take care.
No need to worry. If I cannot subdue even these apparitions, then I'm not fit to be the master of the Garden of Grotesqueries.
If I may, by the way, a request for my humble... record-keeping friend?
Though you have come from afar, I regret to say I have no time to be a proper host. If you ever have some spare time in the future, I ask that you come again.
But if fate is against us, and you are the last visitors to my Garden... I would like you to preserve its sights with your brush and ink.
...I shall do my utmost.
Please, stay safe, sir.
(Smiles and nods)
My my, this shaking is far too strong, as well as this... resentment?
Even though only a fraction of this millennium of hatred and rage has spilled out here, it still has such strength. I dare not imagine what exactly Wang is confronting down there...
But even having come to this point, It is still not yet awake, which means... our designs with the Garden are still in effect.
It is a shame though. I fear the structures within the Tomb are almost completely destroyed... I suppose I shall have to put a bit more effort into suppressing It, then.
...You really know how to cause quite the stir, Wang.
*sigh*... These geists are, in the end, formed from your fury.
I have no desire to entertain such vulgar creations, nor am I interested in collecting them... All I can say is that they will be a little hard to handle.
The proxy looks up towards the once-peaceful town at the foot of the mountain, now devastated and in ruins.
Even a layabout has times when he is angered... I cannot forgive such destruction.
Now, how much of this account shall be considered yours, Wang—
I shall make it whole for him. Reluctantly, I might add.
Ji?
...Wang chased me away, so with nowhere else to go, I came to help you.
Hahahaha... Seriously?
It does not really matter.
I suppose I shall owe you a great deal if you were to help me with this big mess today...
Since when have you been concerned about what you owe me?
Why, I would never. It is simply because you are so magnanimous, our third eldest brother, that you find it embarrassing to settle accounts with your younger siblings.
Hmph...
Shall I help you suppress Sui, or help you look after Mangshan Town?
First, we deal with the geists and protect the town. We will still have time to suppress It. There are two of us, after all. With enough speed, neither issue needs to wait.
Rather greedy of you, no?
Hardly the first time you have seen my greed.
True. If I had not dropped by today, all those exotic plants, fine woods and strange stones you so painstakingly gathered would be ruined, which would be quite the pity.
...Well, I need only come and ask you for more later.
Teal and pink flames join the spreading golden threads, searing away the murky black geists.
What a pain. We just got here, and all these things popped out of nowhere. There's no end to them either, they just keep coming like misua that's been soaking for too long...
Best hide somewhere behind me, Grand Tutor. I'm not too good at fighting, nearly whacked you a few times there.
It's a half-baked plan, but all I can do is—
Still talking about food, kid?
I've added a little something to your pot of half-baked, swollen misua, no need to thank me!
Let's see... Sisters number one and two, the kiddo, who else? No big deal if Eight-o doesn't show up. What about Ji? I've got a little something for him too...
Forget it. Heed me, Dark Devastator!
Quantum Firecracker, Quantum Launcher, Quantum Rumbler, Quantum Cannon, lock onto thirty meters around the kid, and—

Fire at will! Fire everything!
Fire!
FIRE!
SHUT UP ALREADY!
What the hell even is this stupid Dark Devastator thing?!
Shu and I only agreed to help you because you promised you wouldn't make these weird things again!
Made it pretty much the same way as the Twelve Pagodas and Five Cities. This is war, after all, and all's fair in it! That weiqi failure loves saying shit like that.
Who exactly are you declaring war on by aiming that stuff at me?!
Get her under control, Shu!
This really isn't what you promised earlier. Once this is all over, don't forget to make an apology.
Heheh, sorry, sorry...
These firecrackers really do remind me of Dahuang, though...
They are subdued during the Shennong festival, but when the New Year arrives, the firecrackers they light up might be even fiercer than the ones Nian just used...
Right? A family reunion should be more lively—
If you had fired that barrage earlier, it would have fallen at Yu's feet.
Take a break and let Dusk play with it. She's been waiting for a while now.
Me? I don't want to touch that thing—
So should I continue?
Give it to me.
If I let you keep firing that stuff, you're going to burst everyone's eardrums!
Shu! And Nian, and Dusk! You're finally here!
Long time no see, kiddo. Dinner ready yet?
We aren't done with work yet, Nian. How could you only be thinking of Yu's cooking?
One's not gonna affect the other!
Oh, right, we never did give them a heads-up that we'd be meeting here. Hey, Grand Tutor, it should be fine, right?
Extraordinary times call for extraordinary measures.
If you are able to resolve this geist crisis together, then that shall be an act of great merit.
I always knew you weren't the dried-up stick in the mud you look like!
Say, Grand Tutor, do you think our business with Yan has been a good one?
Perhaps it would not only be considered business in Yan's eyes.
Making a friend is far more important than doing business.
Hah, those're some wise words. Next time those old fogeys from the Ministry of Works come to pester me, I can throw them in their face.
Everyone who should be here is here. Let's take a headcount of the ones who aren't here yet...
Whoa, is this Yan's New Year celebration?

This is just like how Master said they celebrate it. Lanterns, banners, and screaming firecrackers... Though I don't see any lanterns. This is the first time I've seen this many firecrackers, though...
Alright, time to land!
Watch out, Nian! Someone just flew over my head, I think they're going to land right next to you!
Who the hell're you?
Based on your gear, I'm guessing you're...
Nihao! I'm Taraxacum! I'm from Sargon, and I'm an apprentice under my master, though usually I just drive the cart!
Oh, right, my master's called Fāng.
Figured it'd be Number Ten. Man, didn't expect a shy guy like him to have a cheery apprentice like you.
I'm Nian, the ninth, which means I'm his older sister.

Let's give you a quick rundown. Those two chatting over there are Shu, the sixth, and our youngest brother Yu. The one on top of the Dark Devastator splashing ink everywhere, trying to melt away the geists? That's our youngest sister, Dusk.
Ji and Yi, the seventh and eighth, are probably by that mountain over there, while our big sister Ling and second sister Jun went off to nap who-knows-where after some drinks.
Then under our feet, there's Number Two, Wang. Strictly speaking, today's party is all on him—
Wait a sec, got too into introducing the family. If you're here, where's the big one-zero?
It's not that my master didn't return, he just... err... He asked me to give you this...
...A steelyard scale?
Target Feranmut about to arrive at critical position! It's still moving forward!
No time to wait for reinforcements from other cities! We attack the moment It takes Its next step!
We beat Them once a thousand years ago, so no excuses!
The ancient titan lurches forward, powered by the confusion and anger of a rousing from age-old slumber.
It suddenly notices, by Its hind claw, a miniscule, near-imperceptible existence.
Don't go any further. Turn back.
(Doubtful rumble)
I know... The weiqi player summoned you.
I don't want to speak ill of my elder brother. He might not have been thinking of exploiting you for no reason, or throwing your lives away.
(Disdainful snort)
You're heavily wounded. I may have ways to heal you.
If you insist on continuing forward, I also have ways to make you feel pain, a hundred-fold, a thousand-fold... Though I would really rather not.
Turn back... I can heal your wounds, and help you find a new home.
Just don't cause any more death and destruction.
Then Master gave me this scale and told me to deliver it to you quick. He hasn't gone anywhere, he's still where he was earlier... looking this way.
Got it, got it. He's still too scared to come, scared this meeting would be the last... But he still wants to help us out a bit, and that weiqi failure.
So in the end, he decided to be real awkward and send you over, while he waits on some mountaintop somewhere.
Man, our family can be such a... pain in the ass.
On the other hand, I didn't think dried-up ol' Tenner would learn some proper bedside manner. Did he pick up that silver tongue in your part of Sargon?
Not really...
Damn shame, then! I'd also like to learn how to speak good better, maybe then my next movie's funding and lead actor wouldn't jump ship halfway through—
What the... There's still people rushing over to start a war?
Everyone turns their heads towards the nearby city, a city that is slowly but inexorably heading towards their gathering.
Can't just stand by and watch as a bunch of jerks stir shit up... How about we see how the Dark Devastator matches up against Yumen?
No, no, bad idea... But getting in a fight like this seems a little... impolite.
Leave it to me.
Oh, you're... you're that... Ch'en Hui-chieh!
You know me?
You were my supporting actress! The one I cast for that movie! Shame I wasn't able to find you afterwards, or most of the rest of the cast, so I had to delay the shoot.
Uhh... Do you mean that movie Lava was talking about?
Li'l Lava told you about it? Great! We've got the money-guzzling Dark Devastator on scene, and now you're here too...
...You have that thing she left behind.
That's why I'm here.
How d'you plan on stopping that city?
With my sword.
It wasn't part of my original travel plans, but I've stopped by Yumen before. I know a little bit about its structure and weak points.
I heard that master swordsmen don't see things as 'things', but rather 'weak points' and 'flaws'. You one of those masters?
You've been watching too many movies.
There are things only you and your family can do. Likewise, I have things I can't shirk from.
Believe in me, like how I believe in you all.
Ch'en Hui-chieh turns to face the nomadic city rumbling towards her.
She can see the weaponry lining the walls, the soldiers atop the observation posts, and the Tianshis with their furrowed brows. She believes they can see her too.
But simply standing here will not be enough.
She takes a small step back. She looks at the entirety of the city's structure. Its treads, the Wind Aegis, the sand sluices, the watchtowers, the gates.
She knows where this blow should fall.
As sword of heaven's plaint...
Bid dawn as cast.
The writing knife in her hand is not pointed at any one part of Yumen, but rather, up towards the sky.
The knife becomes a longsword, becomes a heart-pounding, golden light...
And then finally, becomes a sandstorm that engulfs Yumen.
Sand?!
Has Sui already woken up?! Look alive, everyone—
No, wait, this is...?
The storm that swallowed up Heaven and earth is not made of coarse sand, but rather gentle, golden snowflakes. They fall onto the soldiers' bodies, their hands, their faces.
The snowflakes melt, and out of nowhere, they hear the beating of a drum.
Those memories, near buried away by the sands, suddenly become crystal clear. They remember a Catastrophe, and those who stood within it...
A poet who drank wine and spouted poetry in its face, or maybe someone who made a lonely exit once everything was done...
They remember the emotions that surged through their minds back then. They worked as one, they fought as one, and for a moment, they forget the confusion and puzzlement pressing down on them as one.
They stop whatever they are doing, silently listening to the pounding of the drum. They count the number of beats, count all the calamities Yumen faced across the years, big and small...
Until the eighteenth rousing farewell boom resounds, the entire weather-beaten city is like an old man overwhelmed by memory...
...One who slows to a crawl, then stops.
*huff*... *huff*... At this rate, I won't make it in time to the last few emplacements!
Why have we stopped?! Don't tell me the artillery already—
A power cut?!
The spent Candleholder realizes he has stumbled upon a stroke of luck.
Even a near-instant cut in power would require many short but essential checks on the cannons before they can re-engage, despite the automatic switch to reserve energy stores.
It has bought him precious time.
Why would there be a power cut now? Surely it's not the proxies and Yumen—
A strong wave of emotion sweeps memories to the surface of Zuo Le's mind, but what he recalls did not take place in Yumen...

lines.st: It is possible that the fate of us siblings will lie in your hands, Lord Zuo.
To be able to chat with you on this festive day, even but for a moment, is destiny at work... I hope that in the future, you can see me as a 'man' you can discuss things with.

lines.x: The doubt and suspicion he felt at the time have now become some sort of illogical, post-realization incisiveness—though he knows there are still many things he is confused about.
But he soon pushes down that sense of relief, that sense that makes him relax, even feel exhausted.
It's not only about the fate of your family. There's also my family, Yumen, Baizao, all of Yan...
(Wry chuckle) Why am I thinking about this now, of all times?
This fateful decision is not over yet.
He forcibly tamps down the wellspring of emotion flooding his heart. His hates-to-lose attitude pushes him onward, hastily winding his way to the last few emplacements.
The golden sands that filled the sky gradually disperse. The radiance fades, turning the blade in Ch'en's hand back to an ordinary writing knife.
To the side, Yu stoops down, grasping a pinch of soft, golden, snow-like sand. Through a daze, he looks at the golden sheen, a sheen he has not seen in over a hundred years.
What he remembers is the older sister he last met over a century ago. But as for what Ch'en sees? She sees a parting far away, in the distant, frigid north.
The snowflakes melt away.
Those newly-bright memories slowly fade again, returning to their worn and shabby states.
All that remains is a sigh on the lips.
Finally... here...
This is... the Tomb of Sui...
Despite having some knowledge of it for a while now, actually seeing such an enormous structure in person leaves her shocked.
With Baizao in turmoil, all its soldiers have been recalled to fight the geists lining its streets.
Across from the door is an abyss that none dare look at.
I was pretty eager to accept this mission, but how do I actually get in...
Ya accounted for my speed and intercepted me. I didn't familiarize myself with that map very well, so she probably beat me here...
So now what? Do I just knock on the door?
Allow me.
You are... Shuo.
Long has it been since I was called by that name. Do we happen to be old acquaintances, madam?
I... happen to be a... friend... of one of your students...
And...
I once stole one of your things, but I already returned it... Sorry.
Err, the thing I'm talking about isn't this thing here, I meant—
I know.
You do?
I could hardly be mistaken about the scent of my siblings.
The man notes the clothes on the woman before him, and his face takes a slight turn for the serious.
Whether it be the martial disciplines or the scholarly ones, there should not be a splitting of sects...
But if you wish to eliminate the prejudice in the hearts of others, then you yourself must first have a 'tolerant heart'.
My thanks... for your wisdom...
The young lady carefully and deferentially hands over Jie's token.
Duoxie, madam.
Delivering this drop of life to the Tomb is no doubt Jun's plan. Aside from Wang, she was the closest to Jie, and she wished her here, even if only as a token.
But I fear that she did not adequately explain things to you. Our family will be gathering in the barrens outside, not in the Tomb itself.
What?
But this is still a stroke of good fortune. Before our family reunion, I shall have this life accompany me on a trip into the Tomb.
Surely this isn't a piece that weiqi player is planning to use up when defeating Sui... right?
He worked so hard to bring this out into the mortal realm. How could I betray his intention by consuming it?
It is simply to bring him peace of mind, nothing more.
...Traitors.
You twelve, each and every one of you, plan to betray me, yes?
Indeed. None of us will submit to becoming a part of you.
We have lived as ourselves. This is why we are different from you.
Lived as... humans?
You see yourselves as human, and try to bring delusions varied to bear against me.
You relied on the power you stole from me, ingratiating yourselves with those puny insects, but you still had to humble yourselves, hiding your claws and fangs to make yourselves look harmless.
Is that what you mean by living as a human?!
You still do not understand... You can never understand, for you have never lived.
And I am well-aware... from where your arrogance stems.
With but a glance, you can look from the beginning of time to its end. Though your consciousness has traversed the great void for eons, you have never had kin you could converse with.
You are simply a god declaring Itself supreme over a pitiful plot of empty space.
I know full well what this feeling was like.
Until the day someone gave me a name.
You are nobody's fate... Not the people of this land, or we twelve.
And there is one thing you will never understand: against someone who already has nothing to his name...
...Fate is utterly powerless.
Very well... Since you dare to repeat your foolish challenge, I do not mind shattering your delusions once more.
I killed you once a hundred-and-twenty years ago! I shall kill you again with but a flick of my wrist!
Is that meant to scare me?
I am already accustomed to losing.
I have already lost too many precious, precious things. I have lost the entire board.
My dignity, my honor, my trust, my freedom, myself.
My family.
Did you really think someone with nothing more to lose would be afraid?
But you?
Are you not afraid you will suffer another ignominious defeat, like a thousand years ago?
Is this fear not what makes you hesitate to step forward?
...A jest, at this hour!
You desire in vain to replace me, to steal away my body. Why would I allow you to do as you please?!
I need only stir this body awake first—
The Feranmut extends Its talons, talons as great as mountains.
Countless Feranmuts in countless Tombs extend Their talons, countless talons as great as mountains, and point them towards the same proxy.
It does not matter if this is a dream. So long as that loathsome face is wiped away in all of them, there will be nothing left to stop Its awakening.
What is this...
An incomparably brutal blow slams into Its body. A strength It is familiar with.
It knows, that in this split-second, It has lost Its connection with Its enormous body.
Who could cause such harm to Its form?!
The space shakes, then warps, and tears...
As if a thousand years worth of martial prowess has been channeled into a single strike, ripping apart the edges of the dream.
Why are you—
Always so meddlesome!
I knew you would say that.
I also promised that, were things to arrive at this stage, I will be with you.
Besides, I also wanted to bring her to meet you, to let you know that all your years of planning... were not for naught.
Through the boundary between dream and reality, Wang sees the token Chongyue clutches to his bosom...
And then, their eyes meet.
You should leave...
Live.
Live, and come back to us.
I will not make you any promises.
The distance between them stretches with speed.
Even though he is a Grandmaster of kung fu, a thousand years old, a human body is still only a human body.
The force piercing the veil between truth and fiction quickly dissipates, but the voice of that brother Wang is so fed up with still reaches his ears.
Then I will look for you. I always will.
Suit yourself.
What did he... What did he do?!
But it does not matter! IT DOES NOT MATTER! No matter what he did, it was only for an instant... AN INSTANT!
No longer shall he disturb us! Nor will any of your ludicrous, insignificant... siblings!
This is my dream, with only you and I!
I thought I would have to bring all my strength to bear to defeat you, but it seems you are still weak, still liable to collapse at the slightest of breezes. You only have a single stone left...
I will barely even have to lift my finger... to crush you! To annihilate you!
And those poems, those laws, those martial arts... Such irksome pests. I need only use the strength I saved for you to isolate you from those meddlesome insects!
You are unfit to die by my claws. You are only fit to be cut down by your reflection's blade... Now DIE!
Hah... Is that so?
To die is your end, not mine.
Yes. In all these causal chains, in all these possibilities, in all these dreams, you will die.
These countless repetitions within this dream have eroded away, hollowed out your most frenzied imaginings. And all without you knowing.
Have you not noticed how intricately linked you and I are to this dream you used to trap me?
You have been chained by your hateful, vengeful impulses... Your evolution has reached its end.
And we have already surpassed you.
But your body will persist.
I... will take your place.
And how will you do that? With your isolated stones, and with your own body, so ready to fall apart?!
You dare assume... I only have my stones... and this body of mine?!
The near-dead proxy uses the last of his strength to lift his sword.
The deranged Feranmut gives one last howl of rage.

lines.st: The cold moons split stones; the fierce sun chars boards...
Ashes to ashes, a farewell to the ages.

lines.x: Memories from time immemorial pour down like a torrential rain, washing away Its enormous, empty consciousness.
The remnant consciousness slips and slides about the land of dreams, trying to find a single scrap of something, anything, to use as its foundation...

But those dreams are now as blank as unscribed bamboo slips, with only the grain of the wood to provide texture. There should be people and events written upon them, yet such things cannot be grasped.
...It has lost.
Without doubt.
You wish to seize Its body, take Its place. But what of your siblings?
They will not see me again, nor will they come to fill this void you have left behind.
That said, I will not disappear. I shall always be with you.
No matter who sits upon this corporeal throne, a void shall form. Since you have no intention of filling it, I shall haunt this place eternally...
...To torment the master of this body?
...To usurp Its will, to torment Its mind, are but two sides of the same tongbao.
There will be something to fill your vacancy.
All that is left of you now is emotion. Abundant emotion, but that matters not in the face of this shattered consciousness. If it did matter, Its rage would have filled it long ago.
...Hah. That will not be a worry.
The one replacing you shall be far more terrifying that you have ever been.
The proxy steps forward, leaving the white creation behind him.
The Feranmut, about to drown in Its own hollow mind, lets out a bitter cry.
Soon, even that cry filled with pain and defiance dies down, leaving only an echo, reverberating throughout the room.
(Murky dying gasp)
You are about to die.
...I am going to die?
Who am I? Why will I die?
...You were the most arrogant of dreamers. In your dreams, you pushed evolution to the endless sea of stars.
But this egotistical evolution was cut short by humanity, that humanity you saw as nothing.
And then your anger, your hatred, and your defiance dragged you to your grave.
But at the very end of it all, you died by my hand.
You...
(Murky dying gasp)
You will destroy... the future?
That future of evolution that escapes ruination, and through infinite expansion, touches the face of the void?
It was a dream you would never have been able to see to fruition.
Evolution will not stop because of your death. Your passing will simply be release for a will that has suffered for a thousand years.
If you truly yearn for that future where the stars shine only for you...

lines.st: He lifts up the last of his stones. In his hand, they slowly lose their forms, turning into a blade.
It looks on as he lifts up the last of his stones. He throws them up into the sky, up towards the boundless heavens.
He takes the last of this congealed sharpness and thrusts it into Its eye—
It watches as those black stones become a galaxy that flows towards It, until...
The bestial Sui closes Its eyes.
The bestial Sui espies the future that exists only in Its dreams.

lines.x: ...May you dream well.
The entire room begins to shake, before ultimately collapsing.
The forms of proxy and Feranmut slowly merge together. No longer shall they be parted.
Here, a new 'Sui' is born.
Still hanging in there, Shu?
This is no big deal. It's just that handling this many of Its minions takes its toll.
Hey, Dusk! Fire that way, there's tons of geists there—
Where?
Where I'm pointing!
There's nothing there!
Are you stupid, there's—
Nian is stupefied.
There really is nothing there.
And not just there. It is as though someone has sneakily wiped away the blot of ink staining the ground. The rampaging geists of just a moment ago are nowhere to be seen.
Whoa, whoa, you nearly chopped me in half!
I'm obviously trying to chop that thing in half!
Huh? Where'd the monster go?
Understood.
The Sui Regulator reports that all the scriptgeists have disappeared.
We... We won?
Don't let your guard down—
An earthquake?!
It stirs.
All hands, move to an open space!
The shaking from afar arrives.
The time has come.
The True Lung grasps the jade seal used to start the device. It might just be his imagination running wild, but he feels a connection between the machine and the blood running through his veins.
During this moment of hesitation, a great shadow appears before him, nearly covering the entirety of the Originium's radiance.
Are you... It?
The shadow utters what seems to be a threatening, low roar. Within that roar, the True Lung hears a haggard voice, one he heard decades ago.
No... You are no longer It.
But how many of Its memories and dreams, Its thoughts and feelings have you inherited?
Will you be of aid to Yan in the future, or will you bring only misfortune?
Are you he, or an all-new Sui?
Its pale memories, withered dreams, exhausted thoughts, befouled feelings... They all roil and jostle through my consciousness at this very moment...
But... you are correct.
Sui is dead!
The shadow lets out a long howl that shakes both heaven and earth.
But what you, your ancestors, your nation, owe It, is also what you owe me and mine.
Those Arts winding their way through your body are proof your ancestor shackled us for a thousand years!
Though these memories be faded, these dreams be wilted, these thoughts be decayed, these feelings be rotted away, this rage of a thousand years still burns as bright as the sun!
But you... are not him.
